title: Google Vids adds Gemini Omni generation and personal avatars
friendlyTitle: Google makes it easier to create videos with AI
summary: Google is rolling out Gemini Omni in Google Vids for prompt- and image-based video creation and conversational editing. It is also adding personal avatars, allowing users to create a digital presenter from a selfie and voice recording; generated clips carry a digital watermark.
friendlySummary: Google Vids can now make and edit videos from simple instructions and pictures. You can also make a digital avatar from your selfie and voice to present a video.
whyItMatters: For teams that make explainers, training material or internal updates, the feature narrows the gap between an idea and a usable first video. The watermark and consent-sensitive avatar workflow matter because easier production also makes provenance and permission more important.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This can help people make training or work videos faster. AI-made clips have a watermark, and people should only use faces and voices with permission.
